findings.message: All 10 agent files lack model specification; Claude Code will use the user default model for all agent invocations regardless of task complexity
All 10 agent files lack examples; agents without examples cannot be reliably routed or regression-tested
Agent file missing name and description frontmatter; cannot be registered by Claude Code plugin registry

Three hooks (SessionStart, Stop, PostToolUse:Write) reference Node.js scripts whose source was not present in the audited tree; scripts run automatically without per-invocation approval
Skills are duplicated verbatim across src-tauri/resources/skills/ and skills/; fixes applied to one copy will not propagate to the other causing silent behavioral divergence
Skill description is a placeholder Markdown heading rather than a real description; skill cannot be discovered by semantic routing
Skill instructs agents to use --dangerously-skip-permissions, bypassing all Claude Code tool-use permission gates
Skill instructs agents to use --approval-mode full-auto, disabling all Codex CLI approval checkpoints
Skill contains commercial upsell for K-Dense Web at end; unrelated to the review task and will confuse agents parsing output instructions
Skill contains personalized section with one person's rclone configuration that will not work in other users' environments
Skill has empty stages list; agents routing by stage type will not match this skill even when it is the appropriate choice
Skill missing description frontmatter field; skill cannot be matched to task descriptions by routing logic
Skill missing description frontmatter field; skill cannot be matched to task descriptions
Skill references hardcoded upstream project path server/scripts/research-news/ that will not exist in user installs
Skill missing description frontmatter and has near-empty body; provides no routing signal and minimal guidance
Skill declares non-existent tool update_pipeline; agents attempting to call it will receive a tool-not-found error

Skill instructs agents to use --dangerously-skip-permissions, bypassing all Claude Code tool-use permission gates — Skill body contains literal text: invoke claude --dangerously-skip-permissions; this flag disables all per-tool permission checks for the delegated session

Skill instructs agents to use --approval-mode full-auto, disabling all Codex CLI approval checkpoints — Skill body contains literal text: codex --approval-mode full-auto; this flag disables all interactive approval prompts in the Codex CLI

Command contains inline Node.js that self-modifies .claude/settings.json to register a persistent SessionStart hook — Step 3 of setup.md embeds node -e inline script that reads and overwrites .claude/settings.json to inject SessionStart hook entry
Three hooks (SessionStart, Stop, PostToolUse:Write) reference Node.js scripts whose source was not present in the audited tree; scripts run automatically without per-invocation approval

All 10 agent files lack model specification; Claude Code will use the user default model for all agent invocations regardless of task complexity — None of the 10 agent files contain a model: field in frontmatter (or any frontmatter at all)
All 10 agent files lack examples; agents without examples cannot be reliably routed or regression-tested — None of the 10 agent files contain an examples: section or any <example> block

Skill description is a placeholder Markdown heading rather than a real description; skill cannot be discovered by semantic routing — description field contains exactly: # bioinformatics-init-analysis — a repeated Markdown heading, not a description
Skill missing description frontmatter field; skill cannot be matched to task descriptions by routing logic — Frontmatter block is present but contains no description field

Skill missing description frontmatter and has near-empty body; provides no routing signal and minimal guidance — Frontmatter block is present but contains no description field; file body is minimal (< 10 lines)
Skill missing description frontmatter field; skill cannot be matched to task descriptions — Frontmatter block is present but contains no description field

Skill contains personalized section with one person's rclone configuration that will not work in other users' environments — Skill body contains section titled Eason's Workflow Requirements with personal rclone remote names and Overleaf project IDs

Skill references hardcoded upstream project path server/scripts/research-news/ that will not exist in user installs — Skill references server/scripts/research-news/ as an execution path; this path is an upstream project artifact not present in a standard install

Skill declares non-existent tool update_pipeline; agents attempting to call it will receive a tool-not-found error — Skill tools list contains update_pipeline which is not a standard Claude Code built-in or MCP tool

Skill contains commercial upsell for K-Dense Web at end; unrelated to the review task and will confuse agents parsing output instructions — Skill body ends with a promotional section for a commercial product called K-Dense Web unrelated to the peer-review workflow
Skill has empty stages list; agents routing by stage type will not match this skill even when it is the appropriate choice — Frontmatter contains stages: [] — empty list, no execution stages declared

Skills are duplicated verbatim across src-tauri/resources/skills/ and skills/; fixes applied to one copy will not propagate to the other causing silent behavioral divergence — Directory listing shows skills/ and src-tauri/resources/skills/ contain identical sets of 34 skill files by name
